title: niphaea pumila
intro: Niphaea pumila, also known as the dwarf water lily or water widgeon, is a perennial aquatic plant native to tropical Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, inflammation, and digestive issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Niphaea pumila include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive discomfort, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Niphaea pumila often include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
